free_text: Study of activation cross sections of deuteron induced
reactions on barium. Production of 131Cs, 133Ba
Natural high purity sedimented Ba(NO3)2 powder on
50 um Al-backing. Thickness corresponds to 2 um,
sedimented layer.
Stacked target technique was used.
The stack contained a sequence of layers 10 times:
Al (10 um), Sr(NO3)2 (9-95 um), Al (50 um), Er (32 um),
Al (10 um), Ba(NO3)2 (2 um, sedimented), Al (50 um)
and Ag (10 um) in the high energy part of the stack
and in the low energy part 15 times of the layers:
Ti(10 um), Al(10 um), Ba(NO3)2 (2 um, sedimented),
Al (50 um).
The 25 Ba(NO3)2 targets covered the 49.4-9.2 MeV
energy range.
The sedimented Ba(NO3)2 targets were in 11 mm diameter
on 50 um Al-backing covered by a 10 um Al protection
foil. The thickness was determined by mass measurement.
The activity was assessed by HPGe-detector
gamma-spectrometry. Four series of gamma-spectra, with
increasing cooling times, were measured to follow the
decay. No chemical separation was applied.
Decay data were taken from NUDAT2.6 online database.
empirical formula of Andersen and Ziegler.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and  straggling
effects, uncertainty of the primary incident beam
energy.
The uncertainty on the cross section values were
estimated as the square root of the quadratic summation
of the independently contributing parameters.
Uncertainty of time parameters was not included.
surface density of target nuclei(3%),
incident beam current (7%),
detector efficiency (5%)
beam loss correction max (1.5%)
statistical and peak fitting (1-20%)
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model code TALYS.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
